en.commentary:
The top of this category is a two-brand fight between Dreame and Roborock, and this week that fight is heating up in a way worth flagging. The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds my number one for now because it still posts the most complete all-round scorecard I have: strong suction, class-leading mapping, and mopping that actually lifts and washes rather than smearing. But I am watching the horizon closely. Dreame's newer L60 Ultra PE has started topping some monthly rankings on sheer value, and Roborock's Saros 20 jumps suction from 22,000Pa to a claimed 36,000Pa over the Saros 10R that sits at my number two. Neither successor is in this roster yet, so I am holding the order and letting my own testing catch up before I move anyone.

The Saros 10R keeps second on navigation that remains the best I have used, threading furniture legs and cable clutter without the nervous re-runs cheaper units make. Dreame L50 Ultra stays third and is the one I recommend most on value, delivering flagship mopping for meaningfully less.

The broader story this month is compression. Features that used to mark the $1,500 tier, extending side brushes, mop lifting, hot-water washing, self-emptying docks, are showing up mid-range. That is why my value scores lower down the list have room to climb even when raw cleaning holds steady. For most homes, ranks three through six now deliver flagship results without the flagship sting.

zh-tw.commentary:
這個類別的頂端是 Dreame 跟 Roborock 兩大品牌的對決，而這週這場仗打得更兇，值得標記一下。Dreame X60 Max Ultra 目前守住我的第一，因為它交出的還是我手上最完整的全能成績單:吸力強、地圖建構同級最頂、拖地是真的把布抬起來洗過再拖，不是把髒東西抹開。但我盯著地平線盯得很緊。Dreame 較新的 L60 Ultra PE 已經靠純粹的性價比開始登上一些月榜，Roborock 的 Saros 20 更把吸力從我第二名 Saros 10R 的 22,000Pa 拉到號稱 36,000Pa。兩台後繼機都還沒進這份名單，所以我先按住排序，等我自己的測試跟上再動人。

Saros 10R 靠著我用過最好的導航守住第二，穿過桌腳跟電線堆都不會像便宜機那樣緊張地重跑。Dreame L50 Ultra 維持第三，也是我最常靠性價比推的那台，用便宜不少的價格給你旗艦級拖地。

這個月更大的故事是差距壓縮。過去只有 1500 美元級才有的功能,可伸縮側刷、拖布抬升、熱水洗布、自動集塵座,現在中階機都看得到。這就是為什麼榜單下半部的性價比分數還有往上爬的空間，即使純清潔力持平。對大多數家庭，第三到第六名現在給的就是旗艦成果，沒有旗艦的痛。

highlights.title: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 守住榜首
highlights.body: 我手上最完整的成績單:吸力強、地圖同級最頂、拖地會抬布洗過再拖不是抹開。還是我的第一。

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps the crown this week, and I'm comfortable leaving it there even as the market heats up around it. Roborock's newly announced Saros 20 jumps suction from 22,000 to 36,000 pascals over the 10R, and Dreame's fresh L60 family adds 100C ThermoHub mop washing, so the pressure at the top is real. But those are launches, not proven testbenches yet, and until I've watched them handle real pet hair and edge mopping I'm rewarding the machines I already trust. The X60 Max Ultra earns rank one because it does everything well at once: strong carpet suction, reliable obstacle avoidance, a dock that genuinely takes chores off your plate, and a price that finally feels fair for a flagship. Right behind it, the Saros 10R stays my navigation pick, its mapping is still the sharpest thing I've tested on cluttered floors. The story I keep noticing this month is compression: mid-range models like the Mova P10 Pro Ultra now ship mop lifting and hot-water washing that used to be flagship-only, which is why value scores across the board look healthy. If you want the best, buy the X60 Max Ultra. If you want 90 percent of it for less, the L50 Ultra and Mova P10 are the smart money right now.

highlights.title: Dreame X60 Max Ultra stays the all-rounder
highlights.body: Nothing displaced it this week. It balances carpet cleaning, hair-tangle resistance, and dock automation better than anything else I've run, and the flagship price finally feels justified.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週 Dreame X60 Max Ultra 還是穩坐第一，就算對手一直在旁邊放大招，我還是很放心把冠軍留給它。Roborock 剛發表的 Saros 20 吸力從 10R 的 22,000 帕一口氣衝到 36,000 帕，Dreame 全新的 L60 系列也加了 100 度熱水洗拖布的 ThermoHub，頂規這邊的火藥味真的很濃。不過這些都還只是發表，實測數據還沒出來，在我親眼看到它們怎麼處理寵物毛跟邊角拖地之前，我還是把分數給我已經信得過的機器。X60 Max Ultra 拿第一是因為它每一項都做得好，地毯吸力夠強，避障穩，基站是真的幫你把家事扛走，而且旗艦機能開到這個價位總算算佛心。緊追在後的 Saros 10R 依然是我的導航首選，在雜亂地板上的建圖到現在還是我測過最漂亮的。這個月我一直注意到的重點是規格下放，像 Mova P10 Pro Ultra 這種中階機現在都內建以前只有旗艦才有的抬拖布跟熱水洗，所以整排的 CP 值分數都很健康。想要最好的就買 X60 Max Ultra。想用比較低的預算拿到九成實力，L50 Ultra 跟 Mova P10 現在就是最聰明的選擇。

highlights.title: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 依舊是全能王
highlights.body: 這週沒東西擠得掉它。地毯清潔、抗纏毛、基站自動化的平衡是我跑過最好的，旗艦價位這次總算對得起實力。

highlights.title: Roborock Saros 20 把天花板拉高
highlights.body: 剛發表的 Saros 20 吸力比 10R 的 22,000 帕直接拉到 36,000 帕。我實測前先按住排名，但這種導航加暴力吸力的組合，下個月很可能就是頭號種子。

highlights.title: 中階機一直在追上來

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds my top spot this week, and it keeps earning it on the balance sheet. Strong carpet cleaning, class-leading mopping and dock automation that just handles itself, that mix keeps it at 9.4. Dreame is clearly the brand to beat right now, the newer L60 line is drawing best-overall praise in this week's roundups, and I read that as validation of the whole X60 and L50 family rather than a reason to shuffle my board. The Roborock Saros 10R stays second and remains my navigation pick. Its solid-state LiDAR and obstacle recognition give it the best mapping score here at 9.6, and RTINGS still calls it the best it has tested. If your home is a maze of cables and pet bowls, this is the robot that threads it cleanly. The Dreame L50 Ultra holds third as the value flagship, the one I recommend when someone wants near-top performance without the top-tier price, and its 9.2 value score backs that up. Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone at fourth keeps leading on raw suction, and the Saros Z70 with its arm sits fifth as the gimmick that actually works on socks and small clutter. Value picks like Mova P10 Pro Ultra keep the budget end honest. Nothing this week justified a rank swap, so I held the board. The category is deep enough now that the sensible advice is to match the robot to your floors, not chase the newest badge.

en.commentary: The big theme in July's reviews is flagship features moving downstream, and that shapes how I read the value column this month. Premium mopping systems, advanced dock automation, and better obstacle avoidance are all showing up well below flagship prices now, which raises the bar for what counts as a good deal. The Dreame X60 Max Ultra stays my number one because it still delivers the most complete package I have tested, top-tier cleaning, mapping, and mopping in one machine, and nothing this month knocked it off that perch. The Roborock Saros 10R holds second on the strength of the best navigation and mapping in the field. The move I made is on the Mova P10 Pro Ultra. With flagship-class features now trickling into lower price tiers, its aggressive pricing looks even stronger, so I raised its value score to 9.1. It holds rank nine on total score, but it is the machine I would point budget-minded buyers toward first. The Dreame L50 Ultra stays third as the best-value flagship, pairing near-top cleaning and mopping with a price that undercuts the leaders. My guidance holds: decide whether you want the absolute best cleaning or the smartest spend, then let mopping quality and dock automation settle it. This is a great month to buy, because the mid-tier has never offered this much.

zh-tw.commentary: 七月評測最大的主軸，就是旗艦功能一路往下放，這也決定了我這個月怎麼看性價比那一欄。高階拖地系統、進階基站自動化、還有更好的避障，現在都出現在遠低於旗艦的價位，這等於把「划算」的門檻拉高了。Dreame X60 Max Ultra 守住我的第一，因為它仍是我測過最完整的一台，頂級的清潔、建圖跟拖地全包在一台裡，這個月也沒有任何機種把它拉下來。第二名 Roborock Saros 10R 守著，靠的是同級最強的導航跟建圖。我動的是 Mova P10 Pro Ultra。既然旗艦級功能現在往下滲透到低價帶，它本來就狠的定價看起來更猛，所以我把它的性價比分數拉到 9.1。它總分守在第九，但要我推薦給精打細算的人，我會先指這台。第三名 Dreame L50 Ultra 守住，它是最超值的旗艦，接近頂級的清潔跟拖地卻用了殺進領先群的價格。我的建議照舊，先決定你要的是絕對最強清潔，還是最聰明的花法，再用拖地品質跟基站自動化做決定。這個月很適合下手，因為中階從來沒給過這麼多。

en.commentary: Reviewers keep saying the same thing about 2026 that I keep seeing on my own floors: this is a year of refinement, not revolution, so my list holds steady. The Dreame X60 Max Ultra stays my number one because it does everything at a high level, strong vacuuming, sharp mapping, and mopping that actually lifts dried spills instead of smearing them. The Roborock Saros 10R keeps second on navigation that is still the smartest I have watched, threading table legs and cords without the nervous back and forth. Third and fourth are close enough that I understand anyone flipping them: the Dreame L50 Ultra wins on mopping and value, while the Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone edges ahead on raw cleaning power. I left both where they were because neither had a market event this week to justify a swap. Further down, the Roborock Saros Z70 is the arm-equipped showpiece that impresses more than it earns its price, which is why value keeps it at five. The Mova P10 Pro Ultra and Eufy X10 Pro Omni remain my value darlings for buyers who want most of the flagship experience without the flagship receipt. The iRobot Roomba j9 Plus and Shark Matrix hold the back of the pack, still solid vacuums whose mopping lags the Chinese leaders. No rank changes from me this cycle. When the field is this refined, honesty means resisting the urge to shuffle.

Dreame is rolling out its new L60 family, and Roborock has the Saros 20 pushing suction from 22,000 to 36,000 pascals over the 10R. Neither is in my tracked list yet, so I am holding the order until I have run them myself, but the message for shoppers is clear: successors are landing and this quarter is a strong time to buy. This week I held every rank. The flagships I track are still the ones to beat, and the gap between mid-range and premium keeps shrinking, which is good news for anyone spending less.

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds my top spot, and it still earns it with a rare balance of top-tier vacuuming, mapping, and mopping that no single rival matches across the board. Reviewers keep pushing the whole category forward, but the X60 remains the safest all-round recommendation for a premium flagship. The Roborock Saros 10R stays second on the strength of a 9.6 mapping score, the model I hand to anyone with a cluttered, multi-room home where navigation is the deciding factor. The Dreame L50 Ultra keeps third as the value flagship, delivering nearly the X60 experience with a friendlier price and a 9.3 value score. The genuine news this cycle is the Roborock Qrevo CurvX, which Vacuum Wars measured at a 92% carpet deep-clean with zero hair tangles, performance strong enough to rank third overall in their database regardless of price. That result nudges its cleaning score up and cements its place in the upper mid-pack as the pick for pet owners with carpet. The Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone still leads on raw cleaning power with a 9.6, and the Roborock Saros Z70 keeps its mechanical-arm novelty near the top. Reviewers this week emphasized that the 300 to 1,000 dollar range is where value peaks in 2026, and that is exactly where most of these picks sit. Ranks hold this cycle, with only the Qrevo CurvX earning a small cleaning bump on fresh carpet data.

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds number one, and it earns it by being excellent at everything at once: carpet cleaning, hair-tangle resistance, mopping, and dock automation all land near the top of what I have tested. When a single robot does not force you to compromise, it stays the default flagship recommendation. Right behind it, the Roborock Saros 10R remains my pick for anyone who values the software and mapping experience most. Its navigation scores a class-leading 9.6, and this month the July roundups keep reinforcing that Roborock is the best overall brand for most buyers, thanks to a polished app, proven reliability, and aggressive sale pricing. That combination matters more than any single spec. For value, the Dreame L50 Ultra is still the smartest money in the flagship tier at a 9.3 value score, giving you most of the top-end experience for meaningfully less. The Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone rounds out my strongest picks with the best raw cleaning number on the list at 9.6, ideal for heavy pet households. The category is moving fast, with new models like Dreame's L60 line and fresh Ecovacs value units landing in the July rankings, but nothing this week justifies reshuffling this exact lineup. Buy for your priority: all-around excellence points to Dreame, app and reliability to Roborock, and value to the L50 Ultra.

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps my top spot, and after several more weeks of watching this category I am comfortable holding it there. It cleans, maps, and mops at the top of the field with a base station that handles nearly everything on its own, and that all-round consistency is what a flagship should deliver. The bigger story this month is how fast the middle is catching up. Dreame's new L60 family has arrived with 30,000Pa suction, tangle-resistant brushes, hot-water mop washing, and Matter support at prices that used to buy you far less, and reviewers are already crowning value-focused L60 models as overall winners. That pressure is exactly why the Dreame L50 Ultra at rank three keeps the highest value score in my top tier at 9.3, because it delivers flagship mopping for noticeably less money. Roborock answered with the Saros 20, the 2026 refresh of the Saros 10R, pushing suction from 22,000 to 36,000 pascals at around $1,389. It is a meaningful jump, and I will fold it into the rankings once I have hands-on data, but for now the Saros 10R holds second on the strength of its class-leading navigation. The Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone stays at four as the strongest raw cleaner here, and the Roborock Saros Z70 keeps fifth for its remarkable mapping despite a value score that reflects its premium. The theme for 2026 is unmistakable: the gap between mid-range and flagship is shrinking, so buying smart increasingly means looking one tier down. My rankings hold this week pending fresh testing.

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps my top spot because it is the most complete cleaner I can buy right now. Strong suction, excellent pet hair pickup, precise mapping, and a dock that handles nearly everything add up to a robot I genuinely trust to run unattended. The Roborock Saros 10R holds second on navigation, and its mapping remains the most reliable I have watched thread a cluttered room without getting stuck. For value the Dreame L50 Ultra stays my rank three pick since it delivers flagship mopping for meaningfully less. This week the monthly roundups leaned toward newer mid tier models that are not in my lineup, with Dreame and Ecovacs both pushing fresh value picks, but none of those beats my current top three head to head on the combination of cleaning, mapping, and dock automation. Roborock remains the safest brand call in 2026 on app polish and reliability, and Dreame is the enthusiast pick when you want the most capable hardware regardless of price, which is exactly why a Dreame sits at rank one. Nothing in my field earned a move, so I held every rank and score. The order reflects tested performance, and I would rather wait for a shipping unit that actually outperforms than chase a monthly headline.

en.commentary: Dreame X60 Max Ultra stays my number one and the July testing keeps piling on reasons. It just took the top overall position on Vacuum Wars, scoring high with rated 35,000 Pa suction, an 89 percent carpet deep clean, and a rare 100 percent on flattened pet hair thanks to the pressure plate that seals against carpet. For raw cleaning power nothing else touches it right now. Roborock Saros 10R holds second and RTINGS still calls it their best overall, because the solid state LiDAR and obstacle recognition give it navigation and avoidance the Dreame cannot quite match. So the pattern is familiar, Roborock leads on navigation and reliability while Dreame leads on cleaning muscle and value. Dreame L50 Ultra keeps third as the value flagship, and the Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone stays right there on cleaning and mopping. The Saros Z70 with its mechanical arm remains the tech showpiece at rank five, fun but pricey. Qrevo CurvX and the Deebot X5 Pro Omni fill the reliable upper middle. Eufy X10 Pro Omni is still my value sweet spot for anyone who does not need a flagship, and the Mova P10 Pro Ultra undercuts everything on price. Narwal, Dreame L20 Ultra, the Roomba J9 Plus, and the Shark Matrix Plus round out the board, each fine for a specific buyer. I kept the order steady since this week only reinforced the same top two.

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps my top spot and it earns the crown by being excellent everywhere. Cleaning, mapping, and mopping all sit at the top of the field, the dock handles the dirty work with real automation, and it does all of this while pricing below several flagships, which is why I keep pointing most buyers here. Dreame's strong month across the lineup, with new models moving up the broader charts, only reinforces how sharp the brand is right now. The Roborock Saros 10R holds second on the best navigation and mapping I have tested, the machine I recommend when a complex floor plan is your real challenge. The Dreame L50 Ultra sits third and remains my value champion, delivering flagship mopping and clean cleaning scores for meaningfully less, and the Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone stays fourth with the highest raw cleaning number on this list. Roborock's reputation for the best app experience and reliability keeps its machines clustered near the top, and this month's reviews still name the brand the safe default for most homes. Further down, the Mova P10 Pro Ultra remains my budget-leaning pick for buyers who want strong value over dock frills. My guidance is steady. Buy the X60 Max Ultra for the best all-round machine, step up to the Saros 10R for navigation, and grab the L50 Ultra when value leads.

en.commentary:
The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps my top spot as the flagship that does everything well, strong suction, excellent mapping, and a dock that handles the chores I do not want to. July was busy below it, with Dreame's new L60 Ultra PE earning best-overall value nods in the monthly testing and Roborock rolling out the Saros 20 Sonic with a sonic scrubbing mop system. Those are exciting, but neither dethrones the X60 at the very top for all-around performance yet.

The Roborock Saros 10R holds second, and I still rate its multiple solid-state LiDAR sensors as the best navigation and obstacle avoidance in a low-profile body that clears more furniture. The Dreame L50 Ultra stays third as my value flagship, since it lands near the top on mopping and automation for less money.

The Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone keeps its place for cleaning power, and the broader 2026 story is that the gap between mid-range and flagship keeps shrinking. Features like mop lifting, hot-water washing, and strong anti-tangle brushes are no longer flagship-only, which is great for buyers watching their budget.

My advice this month is to shop the sales as much as the spec sheet. The best value in robot vacuums is often last season's flagship at a discount, and with Roborock and Dreame both pushing new models, prices on proven units keep moving. The rankings hold steady because the new launches add options rather than displacing my proven top four.

zh-tw.commentary:
Dreame X60 Max Ultra 續坐我的第一,就是那種每件事都做得好的旗艦,吸力夠強、建圖優秀,基座還幫我扛掉那些我懶得做的雜事。七月它下面很熱鬧,Dreame 新的 L60 Ultra PE 在月度測試裡拿到最佳整體性價比的肯定,Roborock 也推出帶聲波刷洗拖地系統的 Saros 20 Sonic。這些都很讓人心動,可是綜合表現最頂那一格,目前還沒人能把 X60 擠下來。

Roborock Saros 10R 守第二,它那組多顆固態光達感測器,我還是認為是最強的導航跟避障,而且機身壓得夠低,能鑽進更多家具底下。Dreame L50 Ultra 續坐第三,是我的性價比旗艦,它用更少的錢就把拖地跟自動化做到接近頂尖。

Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone 靠清潔力守住位置,2026 更大的趨勢是中階跟旗艦的差距一直在縮小。拖布抬升、熱水洗拖布、強力防纏毛刷這些以前旗艦專屬的功能都下放了,對顧預算的買家來說是好消息。

這個月我的建議是,看特價的力道要跟看規格表一樣認真。掃地機最划算的往往是上一季的旗艦打折,加上 Roborock 跟 Dreame 都在推新機,那些驗證過的機種價格一直在動。排名穩住,因為這些新品是加了選項,還沒把我那穩固的前四名擠掉。

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds first this week, and the broader market only reinforces why Dreame keeps winning at the top. Reviewers this month crowned Dreame the innovation leader of 2026, and the X60 embodies it with class-leading cleaning, sharp mapping, and a genuinely hands-off dock. Roborock Saros 10R stays second on the best mapping and app experience in the field, which is the thing you live with every day, and Roborock remains my pick for the buyer who values polished software and proven reliability. Dreame L50 Ultra holds third as the value-flagship sweet spot, delivering flagship mopping for less than the very top tier. The interesting market note is that Dreame's newer mid-range units and a fresh Ecovacs value pick are shaking up the price-to-performance tiers below my top three, but none of that dislodged the leaders I track, so I held every score steady. Ecovacs Deebot X12 stays fourth on strong suction and its OmniCyclone bin, and the Roborock Saros Z70 keeps its spot for anyone intrigued by the mechanical arm, though I still rate it on cleaning first and gimmicks second. My guidance is unchanged: Dreame X60 for the best all-round flagship, Roborock Saros 10R when software polish and reliability lead, and the Dreame L50 Ultra when you want near-flagship performance at a friendlier price.

en.commentary:
The Dreame X60 Max Ultra holds my top spot, and after another week of head-to-head reviews I am comfortable keeping it there. It posts the most complete all-around sheet on the board, 9.5 across cleaning, mapping, and mopping, and the dock automation genuinely earns its keep by handling mop washing, drying, and dust emptying without me thinking about it. For a house with pets and mixed floors, this is the machine I trust to just run.

The Roborock Saros 10R stays a close second on the strength of its navigation. Its solid-state lidar keeps a low profile while delivering the best mapping score here at 9.6, which means it slides under furniture that stops taller robots cold. Reviewers this month keep calling it the best-navigating vacuum they have tested, and I agree.

Dreame's L50 Ultra and Ecovacs' X12 OmniCyclone fill out the elite tier. The L50 is the value standout at 9.3, delivering flagship mopping for noticeably less, while the X12 posts the highest cleaning score at 9.6 for homes that prioritize raw suction.

Further down, the Saros Z70's robotic arm remains a fascinating novelty that its 7.0 value score keeps in check, and Eufy and Mova hold the sensible mid-budget ground. iRobot and Shark round out the list, still trailing on mopping. No launch this week reordered the top, so I carried the standings forward.

zh-tw.commentary:
Dreame X60 Max Ultra 守住我的第一名，又看了一週的正面對決評測後，我很放心把它留在這。它端出全榜最完整的全能成績單，清潔、建圖、拖地全部 9.5，基站自動化也真的有料，洗拖布、烘乾、集塵通通自己搞定，我完全不用操心。家裡有寵物又是混合地板的話，這台就是我信得過、丟著讓它跑的機器。

Roborock Saros 10R 靠導航穩坐第二。固態光達機身壓得很低，又給到全榜最佳的 9.6 建圖分，代表它能鑽進讓高身型機器卡住的家具底下。這個月的評測一直說它是測過導航最好的掃地機，我同意。

Dreame L50 Ultra 跟 Ecovacs X12 OmniCyclone 補齊菁英段。L50 是 CP 值亮點 9.3，用明顯更低的價格給到旗艦級拖地，X12 則以 9.6 拿下最高清潔分，適合把純吸力擺第一的家庭。

en.commentary:
The Dreame X60 Max Ultra Complete keeps my top spot, and it earns it by doing everything at a high level in one package. Its balanced 9.5 across cleaning, mapping, and mopping is the profile I look for in a flagship: strong suction on carpet, tidy edge mopping, and a dock that handles hot-water washing and self-emptying without fuss. Dreame's newer L60 Ultra PE is getting a lot of attention this month as a value overachiever, and I am watching it closely, but it is not yet in this lineup, so the X60 holds firm.

The Roborock Saros 10R stays second on the strength of its navigation. Its solid-state LiDAR and obstacle recognition give it the best mapping score here at 9.6, and RTINGS still rates it the top robot vacuum it has tested for consistent, snag-free runs. If you have a cluttered home with cords and toys, this is the one that threads through cleanly.

The Dreame L50 Ultra and Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone tie for third, and both are excellent. The L50 is the value champion of the top tier at 9.3, while the X12 posts the highest cleaning score on the board at 9.6 and a perfect obstacle-avoidance result in independent testing. I made no rank changes this week. The order is stable, so buy by priority: X60 for all-round balance, Saros 10R for navigation, L50 for value, X12 for raw pickup.

zh-tw.commentary:
Dreame X60 Max Ultra Complete 繼續坐我的第一，它靠的是在一台機器裡把每件事都做到高水準。清潔、建圖、拖地全部平均落在 9.5，這正是我心中旗艦該有的樣子：地毯吸力夠強、邊緣拖地乾淨、基站熱水洗拖布加自動集塵都不用你操心。Dreame 比較新的 L60 Ultra PE 這個月被講得很兇，是那種價格越級的黑馬，我有在密切觀察，但它還沒進這份名單，所以 X60 穩穩守住。

Roborock Saros 10R 靠導航守住第二。它的固態 LiDAR 加障礙辨識讓它拿到全榜最高的 9.6 建圖分數，RTINGS 也還是把它評為測過最強、跑起來最不會卡住的掃地機。如果你家東西多、地上一堆電線跟玩具，這台就是能乾淨穿過去的那台。

Dreame L50 Ultra 跟 Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone 並列第三，兩台都很優秀。L50 是頂端這群的性價比冠軍，拿到 9.3，X12 則是全榜清潔最高分 9.6，獨立測試裡障礙迴避還拿到滿分。這週我沒動排名。順序很穩，照你的優先順序挑就好：要全面均衡選 X60，要導航選 Saros 10R，要性價比選 L50，要純吸力選 X12。

highlights.title: 最全能的一台
highlights.body: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 守住第一，清潔、建圖、拖地全部平均 9.5，基站還會熱水洗拖布加自動集塵，順到不用你管。

highlights.title: 永遠不卡住的導航
highlights.body: Roborock Saros 10R 靠固態 LiDAR 加障礙辨識拿下全榜最高 9.6 建圖分，RTINGS 至今仍評它為測過最強的掃地機。

highlights.title: 頂端性價比冠軍
highlights.body: Dreame L50 Ultra 並列第三，價值分數 9.3，用明顯更親民的價格給你旗艦級的拖地跟清潔。

highlights.title: 純吸力最強
highlights.body: Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone 清潔拿到全榜最高的 9.6，獨立測試的障礙迴避還拿到滿分。

references.title: Best (July 2026) Robot Vacuums
The 4 Best Robot Vacuums of 2026
Dreame vs Roborock for July 2026

en.commentary: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra keeps the crown, and the reason is the same one that earned it: it does everything a flagship should and refuses to fall down on any single axis. Cleaning, mapping and mopping all sit at 9.5, and when a robot is that balanced you stop worrying about which floor type you own. This is the one I point people to when they want a do-it-all machine and a dock that handles the dirty work. The Roborock Saros 10R holds second, and its multiple solid-state LiDAR sensors give it the sharpest obstacle recognition on this board, which is why its mapping score of 9.6 leads the category. If your home is a maze of cables, chair legs and pet bowls, it threads through them cleaner than anything else. The Dreame L50 Ultra stays my value pick in the top tier, it carries a 9.3 value score with mopping that matches the flagship, and for most homes it is honestly enough. The Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone ties it on total score with the highest raw cleaning number here at 9.6, so if suction is your priority it deserves a hard look. Fresh July testing continues to churn the mid-market with new sub-flagship arrivals, but none of them displaced a listed model this week. The board rewards balance, dock intelligence and consistent floor results, and the leaders keep delivering exactly that.

highlights.title: Dreame X60 Max Ultra wins on balance
highlights.body: Cleaning, mapping and mopping all sit at 9.5, so there is no weak floor type. When you want one machine that handles carpet, tile and hardwood without compromise, this is it.

highlights.title: Roborock Saros 10R sees obstacles best
highlights.body: Multiple solid-state LiDAR sensors give it a category-leading 9.6 mapping score. In a home full of cables, chair legs and pet bowls, it navigates cleaner than anything else here.

highlights.title: Dreame L50 Ultra is the value flagship
highlights.body: It matches the top pick on mopping and lands a 9.3 value score. For most homes this delivers flagship results without the flagship receipt, and that makes it the smart-money buy.

highlights.title: Ecovacs X12 OmniCyclone for raw suction
highlights.body: Its 9.6 cleaning score is the highest on the board. If deep pickup on carpet and ground-in debris is what you care about most, this is the one to shortlist.

zh-tw.commentary: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 繼續戴著皇冠，理由跟它當初奪冠的一樣：旗艦該做的它全做到，而且沒有任何一項掉鏈子。清潔、建圖、拖地全部站在 9.5，一台機器均衡到這個程度，你根本不用煩惱自己家是什麼地板。有人想要一台全能機加一個能處理髒活的基站，我就是指這台給他。Roborock Saros 10R 守住第二，它多顆固態 LiDAR 感測器給了它這份榜單上最銳利的避障能力，這也是它建圖分數 9.6 領先全品類的原因。你家像迷宮一樣到處是電線、椅腳跟寵物碗，它穿梭得比誰都乾淨。Dreame L50 Ultra 依舊是我在頂級群裡的性價比首選，它帶著 9.3 的性價比分數，拖地能力追平旗艦，對大多數家庭來說老實講就夠了。Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone 總分跟它打平，還握有這裡最高的原始清潔數字 9.6，你要是把吸力擺第一，這台值得認真看。七月最新的實測持續讓中階市場翻攪，一堆準旗艦冒出來，不過這週沒有一台擠掉榜上的機型。這份榜單獎勵的是均衡、基站智慧跟穩定的地板表現，而領先群持續交出的正是這些。

highlights.title: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 靠均衡奪冠
highlights.body: 清潔、建圖、拖地全部站在 9.5，沒有哪種地板是它的弱項。你想要一台地毯、磁磚、木地板都不妥協的機器，就是它。

highlights.title: Roborock Saros 10R 避障最強
highlights.body: 多顆固態 LiDAR 感測器給了它全品類領先的 9.6 建圖分數。你家滿地電線、椅腳、寵物碗，它穿梭得比這裡任何一台都乾淨。

highlights.title: Dreame L50 Ultra 是性價比旗艦
highlights.body: 它拖地追平榜首，還拿下 9.3 的性價比分數。對大多數家庭來說，它給你旗艦成果卻不用付旗艦帳單，這就是花得最聰明的一台。

highlights.title: 純吸力就選 Ecovacs X12 OmniCyclone
highlights.body: 它 9.6 的清潔分數是全榜最高。你最在意的是地毯深層吸淨跟卡進縫裡的碎屑，這台就該進你的口袋名單。

references.title: Best (July 2026) Robot Vacuums
CES 2026 Vacuum Wars Coverage: Stair-Climbing Robot Vacuums, Hot Water Mops, and the Future of Cleaning
Ecovacs vs Roborock vs Dreame (2026)

en.commentary: July's testing round makes one trend impossible to miss, and it is the best news buyers have had in years. Dreame took the top overall spot on an independent monthly list with a machine that undercuts the flagship tier substantially while still posting excellent real world vacuuming, rare pet hair scores, strong obstacle avoidance, and a fully automated dock. Meanwhile Ecovacs installed a lower priced model as its new best value pick, keeping the roller mop, the pet hair performance, and the dock automation from a more expensive sibling. The premium features are migrating down the price ladder fast, and that reshapes the value column across my whole board. I raised the value marks on the Dreame L50 Ultra and the Ecovacs X12 OmniCyclone accordingly. The order at the top is unchanged because the reasons for it are unchanged. The Dreame X60 Max Ultra still cleans and mops better than anything else I have run, and the Roborock Saros 10R still navigates a cluttered floor with the most confidence. Roborock earns a mopping bump for the Qrevo CurvX on the strength of the company's move to roller mopping, which is the single biggest step forward in floor washing since self-emptying docks arrived. A spinning pad drags dirty water around, a roller scrubs with clean water and squeegees the dirty water back out. Once you have watched both on the same kitchen tile, the difference is obvious and permanent.

highlights.title: Flagship features are reaching mid-range prices
highlights.body: This month's testing put a sub-flagship Dreame at the top overall on real world cleaning, pet hair, obstacle avoidance, and dock automation. That is a genuine shift in what your money buys.

highlights.title: Dreame L50 Ultra value climbs to 9.4
highlights.body: It delivers the mopping quality and dock automation of the flagship tier at a price that keeps falling. It is the machine I recommend most often to people who balk at the top of the range.

highlights.title: Roller mopping is the real upgrade
highlights.body: A roller scrubs with clean water and squeegees the dirty water straight back to the dock. Roborock's push into it earns the Qrevo CurvX a mopping bump to 8.6.

highlights.title: Ecovacs is competing hard on price
highlights.body: The company installed a cheaper model as its best value pick while keeping roller mopping, pet hair performance, and dock automation. The X12 OmniCyclone's value mark rises with it.

highlights.title: The top two hold for good reasons
highlights.body: The Dreame X60 Max Ultra still posts the best combined cleaning and mopping I have measured, and the Saros 10R still reads a cluttered floor better than anything else.

zh-tw.commentary:
七月這一輪實測有個趨勢明顯到躲不掉，而且是這幾年對買家最有利的消息。

Dreame 用一台價格明顯低於旗艦級的機器，拿下獨立評測月榜的綜合第一，實際吸塵表現優秀，寵物毛的成績更是少見的高，避障很穩，基座也是全自動的。同一時間，Ecovacs 把一台更便宜的型號扶正成新的最佳性價比首選，滾筒拖布、寵物毛表現、基座自動化，全部從比較貴的兄弟機沿用下來。

旗艦功能往下滲透的速度很快，這件事直接改寫了我整張榜單的性價比欄位。所以 Dreame L50 Ultra 跟 Ecovacs X12 OmniCyclone 的性價比分數我都往上調。

前段的順序沒動，因為支撐它的理由也沒變。Dreame X60 Max Ultra 的吸拖綜合表現，還是我實際跑過最好的一台。Roborock Saros 10R 面對滿地雜物的走位，還是最有信心的那一個。

Roborock 的 Qrevo CurvX 拖地分數加分，理由是這家公司整個轉向滾筒拖布。這是自從基座能自動集塵之後，洗地這件事最大的一次進步。旋轉拖盤是把髒水推來推去，滾筒則是用乾淨的水刷，再把髒水刮回收桶。同一塊廚房地磚上看過兩種各跑一次，差別一看就懂，而且回不去了。

highlights.title: 旗艦功能下放到中階價位
highlights.body: 這個月的實測裡，一台非旗艦的 Dreame 靠實際清潔、寵物毛、避障跟基座自動化拿下綜合第一。同樣的錢能買到的東西，真的變多了。

highlights.title: Dreame L50 Ultra 性價比升到 9.4
highlights.body: 旗艦等級的拖地品質跟基座自動化它都有，價格還一直往下走。碰到嫌頂規太貴的朋友，我最常推的就是這台。

highlights.title: 滾筒拖布才是真正的升級
highlights.body: 滾筒用乾淨的水刷地，再把髒水刮回基座。Roborock 全力押注這個方向，所以 Qrevo CurvX 的拖地分數加到 8.6。

highlights.title: Ecovacs 在價格上打得很兇
highlights.body: 他們把更便宜的型號扶正成性價比首選，滾筒拖布、寵物毛表現、基座自動化一個都沒少。X12 OmniCyclone 的性價比也跟著加分。

highlights.title: 前兩名穩坐有它的道理
highlights.body: Dreame X60 Max Ultra 的吸拖綜合成績還是我量過最好的，Saros 10R 讀懂一片雜亂地板的能力也還是無人能及。
